issues.description: Playwright's accessibility-based selector using ARIA roles. Selects elements by their computed ARIA role.
issues.workaround: Use page.locator() with CSS selectors (e.g. page.locator('button') or page.locator('[role="button"]'))

issues.description: Executes JavaScript in the browser context and returns the result. Basic usage is supported via the CDP Runtime domain.
issues.workaround: Available via kaze's Page API. Complex serialization of non-JSON-safe values may not be supported.

issues.description: Locates elements by their visible text content.
issues.workaround: Use page.locator(':text("...")') or page.locator('*') and filter by textContent in page.evaluate()

issues.description: Locates form controls by their associated label text.
issues.workaround: Use page.locator('label') to find the label, then locate the associated input by id/for attribute.

issues.description: Locates input elements by placeholder attribute.
Locates elements by data-testid attribute (configurable in Playwright).
Waits until the page reaches a specific load state (load, domcontentloaded, networkidle).
issues.workaround: Use page.waitForSelector() on an element that appears after page load, or add a brief explicit wait.

issues.description: Waits for the page URL to match a pattern.
issues.workaround: Poll page.url() in a loop or use page.waitForSelector() on an element unique to the target page.

issues.description: Playwright's iframe / frame support for interacting with content inside frames.
issues.workaround: Not currently supported. Use main-frame-only tests.
Use page.evaluate() to dispatch mousedown/mousemove/mouseup events manually.

issues.description: Network interception and request mocking. kaze has a Route API but coverage differs from Playwright.
issues.workaround: Use kaze's built-in Route API. See src/api/Route.ts for supported options.

issues.description: Asserts the number of elements matching the locator.
issues.workaround: Use locator.count() directly and assert with primitive expect().
Use locator.isVisible() / locator.isHidden() and assert the boolean with primitive expect().
